fields.System.Description:
The Azure AAD Sync Service install instructions on MSDN @ URL https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/azure/dn757602.aspx

Need improvements including:


  1. It does not state to make sure that the server running AD Sync has the SQL Server Native Client Programming installed on it.

  2. The NOTE section is horrible.  It first calls a SQL Instance name a “SQL Partition”, and then it says to not specify this parameter and fails to mention the parameter to not use.


The old documentation @

https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/azure/dn441161.aspx


was accurate.


Specific feedback can be found in the attached feedback.


Markus Vilcinskas <markvi@microsoft.com> is likely the natural owner for this change request.
fields.System.Title: OneView 27826 - AAD: AADSYNC install instructions for using full SQL need improvements from DIRSYNC docs + Rob Greene

attributes.name: RE AD Sync Full SQL Installation documentation needs work.msg

fields.System.Description:
Details: DirSync installation fails when latest version of Microsoft Online Sign-In Assistant (7.250.4556.0) is already installed.  One way Customers can get the incompatible version of MSIA installed is when Customers go through the steps defined in MSDN article “Manage Azure AD using Windows PowerShell”.  The version of MSIA dirsync tries to install is 7.250.4551.0, which is programmatically blocked when it finds something newer already installed.  This is an embarrassing Customer experience for an Enterprise class Service, that provides no actionable guidance on how to self-help.


Failure Strings:

  • The Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant service installation returned FAIL. See event logs for more details information. Unable to uninstall the Windows Azure Active Directory Sync tool. Use the Control Panel to remove the Directory Sync Tool.
  • EventID: 0 Description: The Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant service installation returned error code 1603
  • EventID: 1013 Description: Product: Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant -- Newer version already installed.
fields.System.Title: Update 2684395 to account for DirSync installation failures caused by having MSIA version 7.250.4556.0 installed

fields.System.Title: AAD: Create public KB on “Stopped-Extension-DLL-Exception”

fields.System.Description:
3013032 "Unable to communicate with the Windows Azure Active Directory service" error occurs in AAD Sync wizard
http://support.microsoft.com/kb/3013032/EN-US


IN the text

<system.net>
    <defaultProxy>
      <proxy
        usesystemdefault="true"
         proxyaddress=http://<PROXYIP>:80"
        bypassonlocal="true"
      />
    </defaultProxy>
  </system.net>


The value in the line

proxyaddress=http://<PROXYIP>:80"


needs to be wrapped in quotes like this:


proxyaddress=http://<PROXYIP>:80


I made the change in the internal version but can't release the change externally.
fields.System.Title: AAD Connect: MSKB 3013032 - value in proxyaddress line of machine.config missing leading quote

fields.ECO.CI.CI.AppliesToProducts: ASG/Office 365 Multi Tenant|||C&E/Azure/Azure Tech/AD&S/Azure Active Directory
fields.System.Description:
We need to update this article for Customers running AAD Connect.  The article is fine as is for Directory Synchronization v1 Customers, however there needs to be a separate set of steps for Customers running AAD Connect in this state.


Steps for AAD Connect Customer would probably read something like.


1. Ensure AAD Connect PWRShell package installed

2. From the AAD Connect server run the following commands.


PS c:\Desktop> $adcon = Get-ADSyncConnector | Where {$_.Type -eq "Extensible2"}
PS c:\Desktop> Set-ADSyncAADPasswordSyncState -ConnectorName $adcon.Name -Enable $True
fields.System.Title: Need to update article 2848640 for AAD Connect Customers

attributes.name: RE PWSync failures top issue discussion.msg

fields.ECO.CI.CI.SupportTopics: Routing Azure Active Directory/Active Directory Connect/Set up Sync through Active Directory Connect Custom settings

ECO.CI.CI.TechReviewers.displayName: Jen field; kishord; jesakowi; shravank; willfid; arrenc

fields.System.Description:
@KBWM,  Please assign a strong writer familiar with AAD to make this article update. Please send "Tech Preview" to all listed and republish as Full KB.


++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++


KB 2915221 is frequently used by CSS to resolve password sync issues but it is currently AADSYNC specific.

The focus of this content idea is to add an AAD Connect workaround between now and when AAD Connect supports this directly in the GA + 1 release.

The goal of this CI is to add the PS script in the attached email to a public section of 2915221 and add AAD Connect to the "applies to" section of the article.


Suggest rewording the doc so that the existing solution is positioned as being AAD Sync client specific and the new PS as being AAD Connect specific


Also, it may make sense to change the article title. The significance of the scenario is NOT that you are using AAD Sync as the sync client but that password sync is enabled on the tenant when you sync a subset of users as a result of some filtered configuration. Later, additiona if not all remaining on-prem users become "in-scope", their objects sync from prem to cloud but their passwords don't - until you run "Set-FullPasswordSync" on AAD Sync computers OR the PS requested by this CI on computers running AAD Connect.

This sentence portrays the scenario more narrowly than it actually is - "out of scope for password hash synchronization"


Also, the issue gets portrayed as being "OU filtered" specific and that is not the case either.


A future version of AAD Connect will sync passwords for formerly  out of scope users once they move in-scope  - i.e. running the PS commands is temporary workaround once  you upgrade to the updated AAD Connect client expected in the fall of 2015


Ask the reviewers how transparent they want to be about all of this.

Shravank may have insight on a related password sync failure that we want to document + he will be a savvy reviewer for the original topic requested by Jen.
fields.System.Title: AAD: AAD Connect: Add PS script to sync passwords to KB 2915221

attributes.name: RE Need AAD Connect workaround for KB.msg

fields.ECO.CI.CI.AppliesToProducts: Not Product Specific|||C&E/Azure/Azure Tech/AD&S/Azure Active Directory
fields.System.Description:
A large enterprise customer doing a POC sync of 3.5 million objects waited 1 week to delete 500K objects using the command:

Get-MsolUser -ReturnDeletedUsers | Remove-MsolUser -RemoveFromRecycleBin –Force

Delete throughput was ~ .5 seconds per object


Purpose of this request is to


  1. Document object delete throughput with the command above
  2. Note that multiple copies of a different scripts can be run to delete 11 objects per second
  3. Note that AAD has a 7K write per hour quota and that deleting an object triggers multiple writes
  4. That when deleting objects, using the commands above they go into recycled bin where each deleted object counts as 1/4th the size of a live object against the quota
  5. That one way to perform faster bulk deletions is to move objects outside of a sync group in your identity


Note the throughput of the above method + provide a list of faster workarounds including the following which deletes 11 objects per second by following scripts from multiple windows

Get-MsolUser -ReturnDeletedUsers -SearchString a | Remove-MsolUser -RemoveFromRecycleBin –Force

In the next window:

Get-MsolUser -ReturnDeletedUsers -SearchString b | Remove-MsolUser -RemoveFromRecycleBin –Force
…
Get-MsolUser -ReturnDeletedUsers -SearchString z | Remove-MsolUser -RemoveFromRecycleBin –Force

Once the above is complete then run the below to remove any accounts starting with non-alpha characters:

Get-MsolUser -ReturnDeletedUsers | Remove-MsolUser -RemoveFromRecycleBin –Force

This matches AADs 7K writes per second quota given that an object delete generates multiple writes.


Get-MsolUser -ReturnDeletedUsers | Remove-MsolUser -RemoveFromRecycleBin –Force
fields.System.Title: Azure: Document delete performance throughput in TN, MSDN, AAD and O365 content describing object deletion

fields.System.Description: I also received more feedback from 2 other customers. It shouldn’t say PROXYIP, it should say PROXYSERVER (or similar) since that is what is documented in the article we link to. That is correct feedeback – we don’t recommend to use an IP address.
